binaries.title: hidbth.sys - HID Bluetooth Class Driver - Request-buffer and pending-count leak on a race-gated reconnect error path

changed_functions.name: HidBthSendIrp (sub_1C0005AA8)
key_changes: Removed a conditional override of the return status to STATUS_PENDING (0x103) on the reconnect-failure path. In the unpatched version, when HidBthConnectToDevice (sub_1C0003AEC, queue reconnect work item) returns a negative status AND HidBthDequeueIrp (sub_1C0002018, reclaim IRP from pending list) returns FALSE (the cancel path already owns the IRP), the function returns STATUS_PENDING instead of the real error.

Unpatched assembly at 0x1c0005bde-0x1c0005bea: 'test al,al / mov ebp,ebx / mov eax,0x103 / cmovz ebp,eax / jmp' - the cmovz overrides the error with STATUS_PENDING when HidBthDequeueIrp returns FALSE.

Patched assembly at 0x1c0005bde: 'jmp 0x1c0005c33' - the override is removed; the function always returns the real error status from HidBthConnectToDevice (carried in ebp via 'mov ebp,eax' at 0x1c0005bcd, replacing the unpatched 'mov ebx,eax').
changed_functions.security_notes: This is a resource-leak correctness fix on a race-and-exhaustion-gated error path. When HidBthConnectToDevice (sub_1C0003AEC) fails to queue its reconnect work item (STATUS_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES 0xC000009A when IoAllocateWorkItem returns NULL, or a negative IoAcquireRemoveLockEx status) and the IRP cannot be reclaimed from the pending list because the I/O manager's cancel path already claimed it (HidBthDequeueIrp sub_1C0002018 returns FALSE), the unpatched code returns STATUS_PENDING (0x103). The direct caller HidBthReadReport (sub_1C0005F70) frees its per-request allocations and decrements the pending-request count only on a negative return (jns at 0x1c00060ed and 0x1c00060ff). A STATUS_PENDING return therefore leaks the 'HidB' read-report data buffer P (ExFreePool at 0x1c0006114, skipped), the associated report object (vtable free at 0x1c0006101, skipped), and one unit of the pending-request count. Nothing else frees these for this request on the pending/reconnect path: HidBthReadCompletion (sub_1C0005DA0), the normal releaser, only runs when the IRP is sent down the stack, which does not happen here. The IRP itself is completed exactly once by the cancel path (IrpList_HandleCancel sub_1C000B034 completes it with STATUS_CANCELLED because the list's completion callback is NULL), so the STATUS_PENDING return correctly prevents a second completion. There is no use-after-free, double-free, or double-completion. The patch propagates the real error so HidBthReadReport releases the allocations. The realistic worst case is gradual non-paged-pool exhaustion (local DoS) under sustained triggering; the override is not attacker-deterministic because it requires resource exhaustion plus a precisely-timed cancel of an internal HID read IRP.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
--- VULNERABLE (unpatched) error path @ 0x1c0005bcd
0x1c0005bcd: mov ebx, eax               ; ebx = error status from HidBthConnectToDevice
0x1c0005bcf: test eax, eax
0x1c0005bd1: jns 0x1c0005c3f             ; if >= 0, return prior ebp (STATUS_PENDING)
0x1c0005bd3: mov rdx, r14              ; arg2 = IRP
0x1c0005bd6: mov rcx, rdi              ; arg1 = list/state struct
0x1c0005bd9: call 0x1c0002018           ; HidBthDequeueIrp - try to reclaim IRP
0x1c0005bde: test al, al                ; al=1 reclaimed / al=0 cancel path owns it
0x1c0005be0: mov ebp, ebx              ; ebp = error status
0x1c0005be2: mov eax, 0x103             ; eax = STATUS_PENDING
0x1c0005be7: cmovz ebp, eax            ; al==0 -> override to STATUS_PENDING
0x1c0005bea: jmp 0x1c0005c3f           ; return

--- FIXED (patched) error path @ 0x1c0005bcd
0x1c0005bcd: mov ebp, eax               ; ebp = error status from HidBthConnectToDevice
0x1c0005bcf: test eax, eax
0x1c0005bd1: jns 0x1c0005c33
0x1c0005bd3: mov rdx, r14
0x1c0005bd6: mov rcx, rdi
0x1c0005bd9: call 0x1c0002018           ; HidBthDequeueIrp - best-effort reclaim; result ignored
0x1c0005bde: jmp 0x1c0005c33            ; return with ebp = real error status (no override)

security_findings.entry_point: Internal HID minidriver I/O. An IOCTL_HID_READ_REPORT (0xB000B, decimal 720907) request from the HID class driver is dispatched through HidBthIoctl (sub_1C0005670), which calls HidBthReadReport (sub_1C0005F70) at 0x1c0006... , which allocates the 'HidB' read-report data buffer P and a report object and sends the IRP via HidBthSendIrp (sub_1C0005AA8). The IOCTL code 0xB000B read from the IRP stack gates the reconnect branch.
call_chain: HidBthIoctl (sub_1C0005670) - IOCTL dispatch handler (LowPart 0xB000B -> read report)

HidBthReadReport (sub_1C0005F70) - allocates 'HidB' data buffer P + report object, installs HidBthReadCompletion, calls HidBthSendIrp; frees P/report object and decrements pending count only on a negative return
HidBthSendIrp (sub_1C0005AA8) - enqueues IRP to pending list and arms cancel routine, handles reconnect decision
HidBthConnectToDevice (sub_1C0003AEC) - queues async reconnect work item (HidBthReconnectWorkRoutine); returns negative status on failure
HidBthDequeueIrp (sub_1C0002018) - reclaim IRP from pending list; returns FALSE if the cancel path already owns it
*** override: cmovz ebp, 0x103 returns STATUS_PENDING when HidBthDequeueIrp returns FALSE ***
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Resource leak on a race-and-exhaustion-gated error path (CWE-401: Missing Release of Memory After Effective Lifetime; CWE-772: Missing Release of Resource - pending-request count)
security_findings.description:
HidBthSendIrp (sub_1C0005AA8) sends or queues a HID Bluetooth IRP. When the connection is not in the forwarding state, the function:

1. Adds the IRP to a pending IRP list via IrpList_EnqueueLocked (sub_1C000AEC4), which arms the IRP's cancel routine and returns STATUS_PENDING=0x103
2. Depending on the connection flags and the IOCTL code, queues an async reconnect work item via HidBthConnectToDevice (sub_1C0003AEC)
3. If HidBthConnectToDevice FAILS (negative status, e.g. STATUS_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES 0xC000009A), it attempts to reclaim the IRP from the pending list via HidBthDequeueIrp (sub_1C0002018)
4. If HidBthDequeueIrp returns FALSE (the IRP could not be reclaimed because the I/O manager's cancel path already claimed it), the unpatched code overrides the error status with STATUS_PENDING (0x103)

The patch removes this override - the function now always returns the real error status from HidBthConnectToDevice.

Impact: The direct caller HidBthReadReport (sub_1C0005F70) frees its per-request allocations and decrements the pending-request count only when HidBthSendIrp returns a negative status (jns at 0x1c00060ed and 0x1c00060ff). A STATUS_PENDING return (>= 0) causes:
- The 'HidB' read-report data buffer P (ExFreePool at 0x1c0006114) to be leaked
- The associated report object (vtable free [rdi+0xE70] at 0x1c0006101) to be leaked
- The pending-request count to not be decremented

Nothing else frees these for this request on the pending/reconnect path: HidBthReadCompletion (sub_1C0005DA0), the normal releaser, only runs when the IRP is sent down the stack. The IRP is completed exactly once by the cancel path (IrpList_HandleCancel sub_1C000B034 completes it with STATUS_CANCELLED because the list's completion callback is NULL), so the STATUS_PENDING return correctly prevents a second completion. There is NO use-after-free, double-free, or double-completion. The realistic worst case is gradual non-paged-pool exhaustion (local DoS) under sustained triggering.
security_findings.trigger_conditions:
1. An IOCTL_HID_READ_REPORT (0xB000B) request is dispatched into HidBthSendIrp with the connection not in the forwarding state (list/state struct [+0x4] != 2), so the IRP is enqueued
2. The connection state must trigger the reconnect path: *(ctx+0x5a6) == 0 when processing; for IOCTL 0xB000B the 'lock cmpxchg [ctx+0x5f4]' at 0x1c0005bba must succeed
3. HidBthConnectToDevice (sub_1C0003AEC) must fail: IoAllocateWorkItem returns NULL (pool exhaustion, returning 0xC000009A) or IoAcquireRemoveLockEx fails (device removing)
4. HidBthDequeueIrp (sub_1C0002018) must return FALSE: the I/O manager must have selected the IRP's cancel routine (via CancelIoEx on the HID read) between IrpList_EnqueueLocked and the reclaim call, so the IRP is not reclaimable

These are internal HID minidriver IRPs; an attacker influences them only indirectly (issuing/cancelling HID reads on a paired Bluetooth HID device) and cannot deterministically hit the resource-exhaustion window. The combined condition is racy and not attacker-controllable.
security_findings.primitive: Resource leak: per triggering event, one 'HidB' non-paged-pool buffer (tag 0x42646948, size = report length + 1), one report object, and one unit of the pending-request count are leaked. Sustained triggering is a potential local denial of service through non-paged-pool exhaustion. There is no memory-corruption primitive - the IRP is completed exactly once by the cancel path, and the STATUS_PENDING return prevents the caller from completing or freeing it a second time.
security_findings.evidence:
UNPATCHED reconnect-failure path (HidBthSendIrp / sub_1C0005AA8):
```c
int32_t status = sub_1C0003AEC(ctx);   // HidBthConnectToDevice - queue reconnect work item

if (status < 0)                         // queueing failed
{
    ebp = status;                       // real error
    if (sub_1C0002018(list, irp) == 0)   // HidBthDequeueIrp could not reclaim the IRP
        ebp = 0x103;                     // override to STATUS_PENDING
}
return ebp;
```

PATCHED path:
```c
int32_t status = sub_1C0003AEC(ctx);   // HidBthConnectToDevice
ebp = status;                          // always keep the real status
if (status < 0)                        // queueing failed
    sub_1C0002018(list, irp);          // HidBthDequeueIrp - best-effort reclaim, result ignored
return status;
```

CALLER HidBthReadReport (sub_1C0005F70), consumes the status (identical in both builds):
```c
int32_t status = sub_1C0005AA8(ctx, list, irp);
if (status >= 0)                       // STATUS_PENDING (0x103) is >= 0
{
    *outFlag = 0;                       // tells HidBthIoctl not to complete the IRP
}
else
{
    HidBthDecrementPendingRequestCount(ctx);
}
if (status < 0)                        // NOT taken for STATUS_PENDING -> allocations leaked
{
    (*(ctx + 0xe70))(reportObj);        // free report object - SKIPPED
    ExFreePool(P);                      // free 'HidB' buffer - SKIPPED
}
```

HidBthDequeueIrp (sub_1C0002018) cancel-ownership handshake (identical in both builds):
```asm
0x1c00020ca: mov rax, rbx              ; rax = 0
0x1c00020cd: dec dword [rdi+0x20]      ; list count--
0x1c00020d0: xchg rax, [rdx+0x68]      ; clear IRP.CancelRoutine, read old value
0x1c00020d4: test rax, rax
0x1c00020d7: jz 0x1c00020e4            ; old==NULL -> cancel path owns it -> return FALSE
0x1c00020e0: mov bl, 1                 ; old!=NULL -> reclaimed -> return TRUE
```

Alternate path (0x5a6 set):
0x1c0005bec: cmp r12d, 0xb000b

0x1c0005c0c: call qword [rel 0x1c00181e8]    ; KeAcquireSpinLockRaiseToDpc(ctx+8)
0x1c0005c18: mov ebx, dword [rsi+0x5fc]
0x1c0005c1e: lea rcx, [rsi+0x8]
0x1c0005c22: mov dl, al
0x1c0005c24: call qword [rel 0x1c00181e0]    ; KeReleaseSpinLock
0x1c0005c30: test ebx, ebx
0x1c0005c32: jne 0x1c0005c3f
0x1c0005c34: mov rcx, qword [rsi+0x50]
0x1c0005c38: xor edx, edx
0x1c0005c3a: call 0x1c00018dc               ; HidBthNotifyPresence

Return epilogue:
0x1c0005c3f: mov rbx, qword [rsp+0x60]
0x1c0005c44: mov eax, ebp                   ; returns STATUS_PENDING instead of the error when the override fired
0x1c0005c46: mov rbp, qword [rsp+0x68]
0x1c0005c4b: mov rsi, qword [rsp+0x70]
0x1c0005c50: add rsp, 0x30
0x1c0005c54: pop r15
0x1c0005c56: pop r14
0x1c0005c58: pop r13
0x1c0005c5a: pop r12
0x1c0005c5c: pop rdi
0x1c0005c5d: retn

breakpoints.why: Entry point of the send/queue function. rcx=arg1 (device-extension / connection state ctx), rdx=arg2 (list/state struct), r8=arg3 (IRP). Watch r12d at 0x1c0005b26 - it holds the IoControlCode (0xB000B for IOCTL_HID_READ_REPORT).

The reconnect call site. Step over the call to sub_1C0003AEC (HidBthConnectToDevice) and check eax - if negative (e.g. 0xC000009A STATUS_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES), the reconnect-failure path is entered.

The instruction the patch removes: 'test al, al' after HidBthDequeueIrp (sub_1C0002018). al=0 means the IRP could not be reclaimed (the cancel path already owns it). If al==0, the cmovz at 0x1c0005be7 overrides ebp to STATUS_PENDING (0x103). In the patched version, this test/cmovz is absent (bare jmp).

'test eax, eax' - checks the return value of HidBthConnectToDevice (sub_1C0003AEC). If eax is negative (js taken), the reconnect-failure path is entered. This requires IoAllocateWorkItem inside HidBthConnectToDevice to fail (pool pressure) or the remove-lock to be unavailable.

Just after the call to HidBthSendIrp (sub_1C0005AA8) in the caller HidBthReadReport (sub_1C0005F70). eax = returned NTSTATUS; the following jns at 0x1c00060ed treats STATUS_PENDING (0x103) as pending, clears the out-flag and skips the release path, confirming the leak of P and the report object.

watch: r12d at 0x1c0005b26 - IoControlCode from IRP stack. Value 0xB000B = IOCTL_HID_READ_REPORT gates the reconnect path
ebp at 0x1c0005b9e - return status from IrpList_EnqueueLocked (sub_1C000AEC4); STATUS_PENDING=0x103 when IRP added to pending list and cancel routine armed
eax at 0x1c0005bcd - return value of HidBthConnectToDevice (sub_1C0003AEC). Negative = failure (e.g. 0xC000009A). ebx holds this after mov ebx,eax (unpatched); patched uses mov ebp,eax
al at 0x1c0005bde - return value of HidBthDequeueIrp (sub_1C0002018) (al=1: IRP reclaimed; al=0: IRP not reclaimable -> cancel path owns it -> override fires)
ebp at 0x1c0005c44 - final return value. If 0x103 while HidBthConnectToDevice returned negative, the override fired
rsi - device-extension / connection state (ctx). Offsets: +0x30 pending-request count, +0x50 device object, +0x5a6 connection state flag, +0x5a7 secondary flag, +0x5f4 atomic guard, +0x5fc state flag
r14 - the IRP pointer. IRP+0x68 = CancelRoutine (armed by IrpList_EnqueueLocked, cleared/tested by HidBthDequeueIrp); IRP+0xB8 = CurrentStackLocation
In HidBthReadReport, the out-flag byte cleared when HidBthSendIrp returns >= 0, so HidBthIoctl does not complete the IRP and the release path is skipped
key_offsets: 0x1c0005bc8: call 0x1c0003aec - HidBthConnectToDevice, queue reconnect work item (negative on failure)
0x1c0005bcd: mov ebx,eax (unpatched) / mov ebp,eax (patched) - saves the status
0x1c0005bd9: call 0x1c0002018 - HidBthDequeueIrp, reclaim IRP from pending list (LIST_ENTRY at struct+0x10)
0x1c0005bde: test al,al - *** THE REMOVED CHECK *** (present in unpatched, absent in patched)
0x1c0005be7: cmovz ebp,eax - *** THE REMOVED OVERRIDE *** (conditional move to STATUS_PENDING)
0x1c0005bea: jmp 0x1c0005c3f - jump to return epilogue (patched has unconditional jmp to 0x1c0005c33 at 0x1c0005bde, skipping test/cmovz)
0x1c00060ed (in caller HidBthReadReport sub_1C0005F70): 'jns' on the returned status - skips HidBthDecrementPendingRequestCount when status >= 0
0x1c00060ff (in caller): 'jns' that skips the release path (report-object free at 0x1c0006101, ExFreePool(P) at 0x1c0006114) when status >= 0
debugging_aids.trigger_setup:
REACHING THE PATH:

This is the Windows HID Bluetooth class driver (hidbth.sys), a HID minidriver that works with bthport.sys. The IRPs involved are internal HID minidriver requests (IOCTL_HID_READ_REPORT = 0xB000B) issued to hidbth.sys by the HID class driver on behalf of a paired Bluetooth HID device; they are not raw user-mode IOCTLs.

Method 1 - HID read traffic (requires Bluetooth hardware or emulator):
1. Pair/connect a Bluetooth HID device (keyboard, mouse)
2. Normal HID reads and connect/reconnect drive requests through HidBthIoctl (sub_1C0005670) -> HidBthReadReport (sub_1C0005F70) -> HidBthSendIrp (sub_1C0005AA8)
3. The IOCTL code 0xB000B gates the reconnect branch when the connection is not in the forwarding state

Method 2 - Pool pressure to make HidBthConnectToDevice fail:
1. Create NonPagedPoolNx pressure so IoAllocateWorkItem returns NULL inside HidBthConnectToDevice (sub_1C0003AEC)
2. HidBthConnectToDevice then returns STATUS_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES (0xC000009A)

Method 3 - Cancel race:
1. Issue and cancel (CancelIoEx) HID read operations so the I/O manager selects the IRP's cancel routine between IrpList_EnqueueLocked and the reclaim call
2. HidBthDequeueIrp (sub_1C0002018) then returns FALSE, and the unpatched override fires

All three conditions must coincide; the window is racy and resource-exhaustion-gated, so it is not attacker-deterministic.
debugging_aids.expected_observation:
When the override fires:
1. At 0x1c0005bde: al = 0 (IRP not reclaimable by HidBthDequeueIrp)
2. At 0x1c0005be7: cmovz executes, ebp changes from the negative error code to 0x103 (STATUS_PENDING)
3. At return (0x1c0005c44): eax = 0x103 instead of the error code
4. In caller HidBthReadReport at 0x1c00060ed: 'jns' taken (status >= 0), out-flag cleared -> HidBthIoctl does not complete the IRP
5. At 0x1c00060ff: 'jns' taken (status = 0x103 >= 0) -> report-object free at 0x1c0006101 and ExFreePool(P) at 0x1c0006114 are skipped
6. Result: the 'HidB' buffer P and the report object are leaked and the pending-request count is not decremented; the IRP is completed once by the cancel path with STATUS_CANCELLED. No crash from this path.
7. Observable via pool tracking: growing count of unfreed 'HidB' allocations (!poolfind HidB / !poolused) and a rising pending-request counter (ctx+0x30). Sustained triggering leads to gradual non-paged-pool exhaustion.
debugging_aids.notes:
STRUCTURE OFFSETS:
- Device-extension / connection state (rsi/arg1): +0x30 = pending-request count, +0x08 = spinlock, +0x50 = device object, +0x5A6 = connection state flag, +0x5A7 = secondary flag, +0x5F4 = atomic guard, +0x5FC = state flag, +0xE68 = object-allocator vtable slot, +0xE70 = object-free vtable slot
- List/state struct (rdi/arg2): +0x04 = state (2 = forwarding), +0x08 = spinlock, +0x10 = LIST_ENTRY for pending IRPs, +0x20 = list count, +0x28 = pending-list spinlock, +0x28(byte-offset 0x28 in .c = list+40 fields) cancel routine, +0x30 (list+48) completion callback (NULL for the read list)
- IRP (r14/arg3): +0x68 = CancelRoutine, +0xB8 = Tail.Overlay.CurrentStackLocation, stack+0x18 = IoControlCode (0xB000B)

OWNERSHIP HANDSHAKE (why this is a leak, not corruption):
- HidBthDequeueIrp (sub_1C0002018) does 'xchg rax,[IRP+0x68]' with rax=0: it clears the IRP's CancelRoutine and reads the old value. Old != NULL -> the driver cleared it first, owns the IRP -> return TRUE. Old == NULL -> the I/O manager already selected the cancel routine, the cancel path owns the IRP -> return FALSE.
- In the FALSE case, IrpList_HandleCancel (sub_1C000B034) completes the IRP once with STATUS_CANCELLED (list completion callback is NULL). The unpatched STATUS_PENDING return prevents HidBthReadReport/HidBthIoctl from completing it a second time - which is correct - but leaks P, the report object, and the pending count.
- HidBthReadCompletion (sub_1C0005DA0), the normal releaser of P/report object/count, only runs when the IRP is sent down the stack (IofCallDriver), which does not happen on the pending/reconnect path. So on the FALSE path nothing releases them in the unpatched build.

VULNERABLE vs FIXED:
- Unpatched: HidBthConnectToDevice fails AND HidBthDequeueIrp returns FALSE -> override to STATUS_PENDING -> caller skips release -> leak
- Fixed: HidBthConnectToDevice fails -> HidBthDequeueIrp called but result ignored -> returns the real error status (in ebp from mov ebp,eax at 0x1c0005bcd) -> caller releases P/report object and decrements count

BASE: analyzed base = 0x1C0000000. On a live system use 'lm m hidbth' for the loaded base and add RVAs.

SYMBOLS present in .asm/.c: HidBthSendIrp, HidBthReadReport, HidBthIoctl, HidBthConnectToDevice, HidBthDequeueIrp, IrpList_EnqueueLocked, IrpList_HandleCancel, HidBthIrpCancelRoutine, HidBthReadCompletion.

summary: The patch removes a single conditional return-status override in HidBthSendIrp (sub_1C0005AA8) of hidbth.sys (HID Bluetooth class driver). On the reconnect-failure path, when HidBthConnectToDevice (sub_1C0003AEC) fails to queue its reconnect work item (e.g. STATUS_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES 0xC000009A when IoAllocateWorkItem returns NULL) and the IRP cannot be reclaimed from the pending list because the I/O manager's cancel path already claimed it (HidBthDequeueIrp sub_1C0002018 returns FALSE), the unpatched code overrides the real error with STATUS_PENDING (0x103) via 'cmovz ebp,0x103' at 0x1c0005be7. The direct caller HidBthReadReport (sub_1C0005F70), which is byte-identical in both builds, frees its per-request allocations and decrements the pending-request count only on a negative return; a STATUS_PENDING return therefore leaks the 'HidB' read-report data buffer P (ExFreePool at 0x1c0006114), the associated report object (vtable free at 0x1c0006101), and one unit of the pending-request count. Nothing else frees them on this path (HidBthReadCompletion runs only when the IRP is sent down the stack), and the IRP is completed exactly once by the cancel path (IrpList_HandleCancel sub_1C000B034, STATUS_CANCELLED) so the override correctly prevents a second completion. There is NO use-after-free, double-free, or double-completion. The patch propagates the real error so the caller releases the allocations. Severity is Low: the consequence is a resource leak (potential local DoS via non-paged-pool exhaustion), and the override is reachable only when the non-forwarding reconnect branch, a resource-exhaustion failure, and a precisely-timed cancel of an internal HID read IRP coincide - not attacker-deterministic. IOCTL note: 0xB000B is IOCTL_HID_READ_REPORT (decimal 720907), not a Bluetooth BRB code.
